title: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS)
meta_description: SCSS offers 8.2% interest for senior citizens with ₹30 lakh max deposit, quarterly payouts, and Section 80C tax benefits.

hero.one_liner: Highest interest rate government scheme for retirees — 8.2% with quarterly payouts and Section 80C tax benefit

what_is_it: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) is a government-backed savings scheme specifically for Indians aged 60 and above. It offers one of the highest interest rates among all government schemes — currently 8.2% per annum, paid quarterly directly to your bank account.

You can deposit up to ₹30 lakh (increased from ₹15 lakh in 2023). The tenure is 5 years, extendable by 3 more years. Deposits qualify for Section 80C deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh. SCSS can be opened at any post office or authorized bank. Interest is paid on March 31, June 30, September 30, and December 31 each year.

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: 60 years or above. Retired defense personnel: 50+. VRS retirees: 55+

eligibility.label: Joint account
eligibility.value: Allowed with spouse (but entire amount attributed to primary holder)

eligibility.label: Where to open
eligibility.value: Any post office or authorized bank (SBI, PNB, HDFC, ICICI, etc.)

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Age proof, Aadhaar, PAN, passport photos, retirement proof (for 50-59 age group)

how_to_apply.title: Visit post office or bank
how_to_apply.description: Carry Aadhaar, PAN, age proof (passport/birth certificate), and the amount to deposit.

how_to_apply.title: Fill SCSS account opening form
how_to_apply.description: Fill personal details, nominee details, and deposit amount (min ₹1,000, max ₹30 lakh, in multiples of ₹1,000).

how_to_apply.title: Make deposit
how_to_apply.description: Pay via cheque, DD, or cash (up to ₹1 lakh). For VRS/retired defense, deposit must be made within 3 months of retirement.

how_to_apply.title: Receive passbook
how_to_apply.description: Get SCSS passbook. Interest will be credited quarterly to your linked bank account.

important_alert.text: Interest above ₹50,000 per year is subject to TDS. Submit Form 15H at the beginning of each financial year to avoid TDS if your total income is below taxable limit.

important_dates.label: Tenure
important_dates.value: 5 years (extendable by 3 years within 1 year of maturity)

important_dates.label: Premature closure
important_dates.value: Allowed after 1 year with 1.5% penalty; after 2 years with 1% penalty

faqs.question: Is SCSS interest taxable?
faqs.answer: Yes. Interest is taxable as per your income tax slab. However, the deposit amount qualifies for Section 80C deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh. TDS is deducted if interest exceeds ₹50,000/year.

faqs.question: Can I have multiple SCSS accounts?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can have multiple accounts across post offices and banks. But total deposit across all accounts cannot exceed ₹30 lakh.

faqs.question: What if I need money before 5 years?
faqs.answer: Premature withdrawal allowed after 1 year: 1.5% of deposit deducted as penalty. After 2 years: 1% penalty. Within first year: not allowed except in exceptional circumstances.

faqs.question: Can NRIs open SCSS?
faqs.answer: No. SCSS is only for resident Indian citizens. If you become NRI after opening, the account can continue till maturity but at a reduced interest rate.
